title: Is AI Actually Fair?
modules: Data as a Mirror of the Past
The People Who Build These Systems
When Fair for Some Means Unfair for Others
synopsis: A new AI system is being used to decide which students get extra tutoring resources at your school — and the patterns are troubling. You must investigate, challenge assumptions, and ultimately recommend whether the system should be kept, fixed, or scrapped.
rationale: Young people aged 14–16 are beginning to encounter AI-driven decisions in education, employment, and social platforms, but few have the frameworks to identify or challenge bias embedded in systems that appear objective.
learning_outcome: Students can conduct a structured audit of an AI system for potential bias, identify the source of that bias in data or design, and produce a written recommendation for how the system should be changed or governed.
